name: Community Financial
description_short: Community Financial Services FCU is a federally chartered credit union offering checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and mortgages to members through online and mobile banking.
description_long:
Community Financial Services FCU (CFSFCU) operates as a member-owned financial cooperative providing comprehensive banking services. The credit union maintains a traditional banking model with both digital and in-person access, positioning itself as a community-focused alternative to commercial banks. Founded to serve members rather than shareholders, the institution emphasizes personalized service alongside modern technology.

CFSFCU offers a broad range of financial products including deposit accounts (checking and savings), consumer loans (auto, personal, HELOC), credit cards with balance transfer options, and mortgage services for home purchase and refinance. Members access accounts through online banking, a mobile app (TouchBanking), bill pay functionality, and remote deposit capture. The credit union participates in the Allpoint ATM network, providing surcharge-free access to 43,000 ATMs worldwide. Loan products advertise competitive rates: used auto at 6.25% APR, new auto at 5.75% APR, HELOC at 8.75% APR, and credit cards starting at 18.00% APR.

As a credit union, CFSFCU distinguishes itself through member ownership structure, not-for-profit status, and emphasis on personalized service. The organization provides digital conveniences modern consumers expect while maintaining accessible customer service via phone (908-245-1650). The recent website redesign signals ongoing investment in user experience. Balance transfer credit card rates as low as 5.9% APR indicate competitive positioning in the credit card market.

CFSFCU is a legitimate federally insured banking institution suitable for members seeking traditional banking services with digital convenience. The credit union model provides member-centric governance and typically lower fees than commercial banks. However, prospective members should verify membership eligibility requirements, as credit unions typically restrict membership by geography, employment, or affiliation. Loan approval remains subject to credit review and stated restrictions.
services: Online banking with account balance checks and activity viewing
Bill pay functionality for paying bills online
Mobile banking app (TouchBanking) with online enrollment
Remote deposit capture using smartphone camera
Surcharge-free ATM access through Allpoint network (43,000 ATMs)
Used auto loans (as low as 6.25% APR)
New auto loans (as low as 5.75% APR)
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC as low as 8.75% APR)
Credit cards with balance transfer options (5.9% APR promotional)
pros: 43,000 surcharge-free ATMs through Allpoint network worldwide
Mobile app with remote deposit capture feature for check deposits via smartphone
Online bill pay integrated into online banking platform
Competitive auto loan rates (5.75% APR for new auto, 6.25% APR for used auto)
Balance transfer credit card option starting at 5.9% APR
Full-service mortgage programs for purchase and refinance
Mobile-first digital banking with TouchBanking app available on iOS and Android
cons: No information on website regarding membership eligibility or account opening requirements
Limited transparency on fees, minimum balance requirements, or account terms
Credit card APR range (18.00% standard rate) reflects standard market rates with no competitive advantage shown
Website lacks details on customer service availability, response times, or support quality
No information on deposit insurance coverage limits or NCUA membership details
best_for: Members seeking digital banking convenience with traditional credit union service
Consumers with good credit looking for competitive auto loan and balance transfer rates
People needing surcharge-free ATM access across a large national network
Homebuyers interested in mortgage products with personalized credit union service
diagnosis: Community Financial Services FCU is best for members seeking comprehensive banking services with digital convenience and competitive loan rates typical of credit unions. The main caveat is that credit union membership typically requires eligibility verification (geographic, employment, or affiliation-based), which the website does not detail—prospective customers must verify membership requirements before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
